content:
The patient is a 55-year-old male who presented to the emergency department with acute anterior wall ST-elevation myocardial infarction. He reported sudden onset of severe substernal chest pain radiating to the left arm and jaw while at home, accompanied by diaphoresis and dyspnea. Initial electrocardiogram demonstrated ST elevation in the precordial leads V1 through V4 with reciprocal changes inferiorly. Troponin I was markedly elevated at 8.2 ng/mL. The patient was immediately taken to the cardiac catheterization laboratory where angiography revealed a thrombotic occlusion of the left anterior descending coronary artery. Primary percutaneous coronary intervention was performed with successful placement of a drug-eluting stent to the LAD with restoration of TIMI 3 flow. Post-intervention angiography showed excellent stent apposition with minimal residual stenosis.

Following the acute intervention, the patient was admitted to the coronary care unit for continuous hemodynamic monitoring and medical optimization. Transthoracic echocardiography on hospital day two revealed an ejection fraction of 40 percent with anterior wall hypokinesis and mild mitral regurgitation. B-type natriuretic peptide was elevated at 385 pg/mL. The patient was initiated on guideline-directed medical therapy including high-intensity statin, dual antiplatelet therapy with aspirin and ticagrelor, metoprolol, lisinopril, and eplerenone. He remained hemodynamically stable throughout the remainder of his hospitalization with no recurrent chest pain, arrhythmias, or mechanical complications. Serial troponins peaked at 12.4 ng/mL and subsequently trended downward.

The patient was discharged on hospital day four in stable condition with close outpatient cardiology follow-up arranged for repeat echocardiography in six weeks to reassess ventricular function and guide further therapy optimization. He was counseled extensively on medication adherence, cardiac rehabilitation enrollment, and lifestyle modifications including smoking cessation and dietary sodium restriction.
